questions.stem: The book _____ by many students in our school last year.
questions.explanation: 本题考查一般过去时的被动语态。由时间状语last year（去年）可知要用过去时；主语The book与read之间是被动关系（书被读），所以用被动语态was/were + 过去分词。book是单数，故用was read。A是主动语态第三人称单数；B是一般现在时被动语态，时态不符；D是现在分词，不能单独作谓语。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: —Where is Mr. Green? —He _____ to the library. He _____ there ten minutes ago.
questions.explanation: 本题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的区别。第一空：has gone to表示'去了某地（现在不在说话现场）'，符合语境（Mr. Green现在不在这里）；has been to表示'去过某地（已回来）'，不符合。第二空：ten minutes ago是具体的过去时间状语，只能用一般过去时went。口诀：有ago用过去，have/has gone表去了未回。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The girl _____ is wearing a red dress is my best friend.
questions.explanation: 本题考查定语从句关系代词的选择。先行词是The girl（指人），且关系代词在从句中作主语（is wearing），所以用who。which指物；whom指人但只能作宾语；whose表示'谁的'，在从句中作定语。判断技巧：先行词是人且从句缺主语，选who。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: —Is that Mr. Wang knocking at the door? —It _____ be him. He has gone to Shanghai on business.
questions.explanation: 本题考查情态动词表推测的用法。根据后句'He has gone to Shanghai on business（他去上海出差了）'可知，敲门的'不可能'是他。can't表示'不可能'（否定推测）；must表示'一定是'（肯定推测）；might表示'可能是'（不确定）；should表示'应该是'。解题关键：抓住has gone to（去了未回）这一关键信息，确定是否定推测。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: We should _____ good habits of reading every day.
questions.explanation: 本题考查固定搭配develop habits（养成习惯）。develop表示'培养、养成'，develop good habits是标准搭配。make表示'制作'；do表示'做'；take表示'拿、花费'。常见搭配记忆：develop/form good habits（养成好习惯），get rid of bad habits（改掉坏习惯）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The task is too difficult. I think it is _____ for a 10-year-old child to finish it alone.
questions.explanation: 本题考查形容词辨析及上下文理解。前句说'任务太难了'，所以后句应该是'对10岁孩子来说不可能独自完成'。impossible表示'不可能的'；hard虽然也表示'困难的'，但与前句difficult重复，且逻辑上不如impossible递进；easy（容易的）与句意相反；necessary（必要的）不符合语境。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The news is quite _____. I don't believe it at all.
questions.explanation: 本题考查构词法（前缀un-）。根据后句'我根本不相信'可知消息是'难以置信的'。unbelievable = un-（不）+ believable（可信的），表示'难以置信的'。believable（可信的）与句意相反；believed是过去分词；believing是现在分词。前缀un-常表示否定：unhappy, unhealthy, unable等。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our class started a special project — creating a school garden. Everyone was excited about it because we could learn about nature while helping the environment.

First, we (8)______ a suitable place behind the classroom building. It was an empty corner that no one used. Then we cleaned the area and removed the rubbish. Some students brought (9)______ from home, such as tomatoes and carrots. We worked together to plant them carefully.

The most challenging part was watering the plants every day. At first, some plants didn't grow well and we felt (10)______. However, our science teacher encouraged us to keep trying. She explained that plants need time and care. Two weeks later, we saw small green leaves appearing. Everyone cheered with joy.

Now our garden looks beautiful. It not only makes our school greener but also teaches us responsibility. When we see the fresh vegetables, we feel proud that we (11)______ something meaningful. This project has shown us that small actions (12)______ make a big difference to our environment.
questions.explanation: 本题考查语境推理。根据上下文'一个没人使用的空角落'可知，首先是'选择'了一个合适的地方。chose（choose的过去式）表示'选择'；built表示'建造'，但后文说'然后我们才清理区域'，说明还没开始建造；drew表示'画'；bought表示'买'，地方不需要买。逻辑顺序：先选址，再清理，再种植。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our class started a special project — creating a school garden. Everyone was excited about it because we could learn about nature while helping the environment.

First, we (8)______ a suitable place behind the classroom building. It was an empty corner that no one used. Then we cleaned the area and removed the rubbish. Some students brought (9)______ from home, such as tomatoes and carrots. We worked together to plant them carefully.

The most challenging part was watering the plants every day. At first, some plants didn't grow well and we felt (10)______. However, our science teacher encouraged us to keep trying. She explained that plants need time and care. Two weeks later, we saw small green leaves appearing. Everyone cheered with joy.

Now our garden looks beautiful. It not only makes our school greener but also teaches us responsibility. When we see the fresh vegetables, we feel proud that we (11)______ something meaningful. This project has shown us that small actions (12)______ make a big difference to our environment.
questions.explanation: 本题考查语境线索。根据后文举例'such as tomatoes and carrots（比如西红柿和胡萝卜）'以及'plant them（种植它们）'可知，学生从家里带来的是'种子'。seeds表示'种子'；books（书）、toys（玩具）、clothes（衣服）都不能种植。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our class started a special project — creating a school garden. Everyone was excited about it because we could learn about nature while helping the environment.

First, we (8)______ a suitable place behind the classroom building. It was an empty corner that no one used. Then we cleaned the area and removed the rubbish. Some students brought (9)______ from home, such as tomatoes and carrots. We worked together to plant them carefully.

The most challenging part was watering the plants every day. At first, some plants didn't grow well and we felt (10)______. However, our science teacher encouraged us to keep trying. She explained that plants need time and care. Two weeks later, we saw small green leaves appearing. Everyone cheered with joy.

Now our garden looks beautiful. It not only makes our school greener but also teaches us responsibility. When we see the fresh vegetables, we feel proud that we (11)______ something meaningful. This project has shown us that small actions (12)______ make a big difference to our environment.
questions.explanation: 本题考查情感线索。前文说'some plants didn't grow well（一些植物长得不好）'，所以学生们感到'担心'。worried表示'担心的'；happy（开心的）与语境相反；surprised（惊讶的）通常指意外的事；bored（无聊的）不符合对植物生长的关心。后文'However, teacher encouraged us'也印证了前文是负面情绪。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our class started a special project — creating a school garden. Everyone was excited about it because we could learn about nature while helping the environment.

First, we (8)______ a suitable place behind the classroom building. It was an empty corner that no one used. Then we cleaned the area and removed the rubbish. Some students brought (9)______ from home, such as tomatoes and carrots. We worked together to plant them carefully.

The most challenging part was watering the plants every day. At first, some plants didn't grow well and we felt (10)______. However, our science teacher encouraged us to keep trying. She explained that plants need time and care. Two weeks later, we saw small green leaves appearing. Everyone cheered with joy.

Now our garden looks beautiful. It not only makes our school greener but also teaches us responsibility. When we see the fresh vegetables, we feel proud that we (11)______ something meaningful. This project has shown us that small actions (12)______ make a big difference to our environment.
questions.explanation: 本题考查时态辨析。句意：当我们看到新鲜蔬菜时，我们为'已经做了'有意义的事情感到自豪。强调过去做的事情对现在造成的影响和结果（现在看到蔬菜），用现在完成时have done。一般过去时did只强调过去动作，与现在联系不紧密；do是一般现在时；are doing是现在进行时。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our class started a special project — creating a school garden. Everyone was excited about it because we could learn about nature while helping the environment.

First, we (8)______ a suitable place behind the classroom building. It was an empty corner that no one used. Then we cleaned the area and removed the rubbish. Some students brought (9)______ from home, such as tomatoes and carrots. We worked together to plant them carefully.

The most challenging part was watering the plants every day. At first, some plants didn't grow well and we felt (10)______. However, our science teacher encouraged us to keep trying. She explained that plants need time and care. Two weeks later, we saw small green leaves appearing. Everyone cheered with joy.

Now our garden looks beautiful. It not only makes our school greener but also teaches us responsibility. When we see the fresh vegetables, we feel proud that we (11)______ something meaningful. This project has shown us that small actions (12)______ make a big difference to our environment.
questions.explanation: 本题考查情态动词表能力/可能性。句意：这个项目向我们展示了小行动'能够'对环境产生大影响。can表示'能够、可以'，强调可能性；must（必须）语气太强；need（需要）通常作实义动词；should（应该）强调建议。此处是客观陈述可能性，用can best。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
When Boyan Slat was 16, he was diving in Greece. He was shocked to see more plastic bags than fish in the water. This experience made him decide to do something about ocean pollution.

At first, many people didn't believe a teenager could solve such a big problem. But Boyan didn't give up. He spent years studying engineering and designing a special machine. The machine uses long floating barriers (浮动屏障) to catch plastic while letting fish swim underneath safely.

The system works with the ocean's natural currents (洋流). It doesn't need any energy, which makes it cheap and eco-friendly. Boyan's team has already cleaned up thousands of tons of plastic from the Great Pacific Garbage Patch (太平洋垃圾带), the world's largest collection of ocean rubbish.

"The technology is important, but changing people's minds is even more crucial," Boyan said. He hopes his work will make everyone think twice before using single-use plastics.
questions.stem: What made Boyan decide to clean up the ocean?
options: A. He learned about it in school.
B. He saw too much plastic while diving.
C. His parents asked him to do it.
D. He wanted to become famous.
questions.explanation: 本题考查细节理解。根据第一段'When Boyan Slat was 16, he was diving in Greece. He was shocked to see more plastic bags than fish in the water. This experience made him decide to do something about ocean pollution.'可知，他是在希腊潜水时看到水里的塑料袋比鱼还多，才决定清理海洋的。A（学校学习）、C（父母要求）、D（想出名）文中均未提及。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.group_passage:
When Boyan Slat was 16, he was diving in Greece. He was shocked to see more plastic bags than fish in the water. This experience made him decide to do something about ocean pollution.

At first, many people didn't believe a teenager could solve such a big problem. But Boyan didn't give up. He spent years studying engineering and designing a special machine. The machine uses long floating barriers (浮动屏障) to catch plastic while letting fish swim underneath safely.

The system works with the ocean's natural currents (洋流). It doesn't need any energy, which makes it cheap and eco-friendly. Boyan's team has already cleaned up thousands of tons of plastic from the Great Pacific Garbage Patch (太平洋垃圾带), the world's largest collection of ocean rubbish.

"The technology is important, but changing people's minds is even more crucial," Boyan said. He hopes his work will make everyone think twice before using single-use plastics.
questions.stem: Why did Boyan spend years designing the machine?
options: A. Because he wanted to travel around the world.
B. Because he wanted to find an effective way to clean the ocean.
C. Because he needed to finish his school project.
D. Because he enjoyed diving in the ocean.
questions.explanation: 本题考查推理判断。根据第二段'At first, many people didn't believe a teenager could solve such a big problem. But Boyan didn't give up. He spent years studying engineering and designing a special machine.'以及第三段介绍机器如何有效工作可知，他花多年时间是为了找到解决海洋污染的有效方法。A（环游世界）、C（学校作业）、D（喜欢潜水）均与文意不符。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.group_passage:
When Boyan Slat was 16, he was diving in Greece. He was shocked to see more plastic bags than fish in the water. This experience made him decide to do something about ocean pollution.

At first, many people didn't believe a teenager could solve such a big problem. But Boyan didn't give up. He spent years studying engineering and designing a special machine. The machine uses long floating barriers (浮动屏障) to catch plastic while letting fish swim underneath safely.

The system works with the ocean's natural currents (洋流). It doesn't need any energy, which makes it cheap and eco-friendly. Boyan's team has already cleaned up thousands of tons of plastic from the Great Pacific Garbage Patch (太平洋垃圾带), the world's largest collection of ocean rubbish.

"The technology is important, but changing people's minds is even more crucial," Boyan said. He hopes his work will make everyone think twice before using single-use plastics.
questions.stem: The word "crucial" in the last paragraph probably means ______.
questions.explanation: 本题考查词义猜测。根据上下文'The technology is important, but changing people's minds is even more crucial'（技术很重要，但改变人们的想法更crucial），由even more（更）可知crucial应该是important的同义或近义词，且程度更深。difficult（困难的）、unimportant（不重要的）、impossible（不可能的）均不符合语境。词义猜测技巧：找同义/反义线索，本句中important就是线索词。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.group_passage:
When Boyan Slat was 16, he was diving in Greece. He was shocked to see more plastic bags than fish in the water. This experience made him decide to do something about ocean pollution.

At first, many people didn't believe a teenager could solve such a big problem. But Boyan didn't give up. He spent years studying engineering and designing a special machine. The machine uses long floating barriers (浮动屏障) to catch plastic while letting fish swim underneath safely.

The system works with the ocean's natural currents (洋流). It doesn't need any energy, which makes it cheap and eco-friendly. Boyan's team has already cleaned up thousands of tons of plastic from the Great Pacific Garbage Patch (太平洋垃圾带), the world's largest collection of ocean rubbish.

"The technology is important, but changing people's minds is even more crucial," Boyan said. He hopes his work will make everyone think twice before using single-use plastics.
questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. The History of Ocean Pollution
B. How to Design a Cleaning Machine
C. A Teenager's Fight Against Ocean Plastic
D. The Importance of Swimming Safety
questions.explanation: 本题考查主旨大意/最佳标题。文章主要讲述16岁少年Boyan Slat看到海洋塑料污染后，设计机器清理海洋垃圾的故事。A（海洋污染历史）只是背景；B（如何设计清理机器）只是细节；D（游泳安全重要性）与主题无关。C（一个少年对抗海洋塑料的斗争）既点明了人物（teenager），又点明了主题（ocean plastic），最适合做标题。主旨题技巧：抓住who（Boyan）和what（clean ocean plastic）两个要素。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

questions.group_passage:
Reading is like exercising for your brain. It opens up new worlds and improves your language skills. (17)_____, many students find it hard to read regularly. Here are some tips to help you.

(18)_____, choose books that interest you. If you love science, read about discoveries and inventions. Don't force yourself to read boring books just because they are "classic."

Next, set aside a specific time for reading every day. (19)_____, reading for just 20 minutes before bed can help you relax and sleep better. Consistency is more important than reading for long hours once in a while.

(20)_____, discuss what you read with friends or family. Sharing ideas makes reading more fun and helps you understand the book better.
questions.explanation: 本题考查段落衔接与逻辑关系。前两句讲阅读的好处（锻炼大脑、开阔世界），后句说'很多学生发现坚持阅读很难'，前后是转折关系，所以用However（然而）。First（首先）用于列举；In addition（此外）表递进；As a result（结果）表因果；For example（例如）表举例。解题关键：识别前后句的逻辑转折关系。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Reading is like exercising for your brain. It opens up new worlds and improves your language skills. (17)_____, many students find it hard to read regularly. Here are some tips to help you.

(18)_____, choose books that interest you. If you love science, read about discoveries and inventions. Don't force yourself to read boring books just because they are "classic."

Next, set aside a specific time for reading every day. (19)_____, reading for just 20 minutes before bed can help you relax and sleep better. Consistency is more important than reading for long hours once in a while.

(20)_____, discuss what you read with friends or family. Sharing ideas makes reading more fun and helps you understand the book better.
questions.explanation: 本题考查段落衔接与顺序关系。根据后文的'Next（接下来）'和'(20)空'可知，这里在列举建议，且是第一条，所以用First（首先）。这是典型的列举顺序：First... Next... Finally...（或In addition...）。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Reading is like exercising for your brain. It opens up new worlds and improves your language skills. (17)_____, many students find it hard to read regularly. Here are some tips to help you.

(18)_____, choose books that interest you. If you love science, read about discoveries and inventions. Don't force yourself to read boring books just because they are "classic."

Next, set aside a specific time for reading every day. (19)_____, reading for just 20 minutes before bed can help you relax and sleep better. Consistency is more important than reading for long hours once in a while.

(20)_____, discuss what you read with friends or family. Sharing ideas makes reading more fun and helps you understand the book better.
questions.explanation: 本题考查段落衔接与例证关系。前句说'每天留出特定时间阅读'，后句说'睡前读20分钟可以帮助放松和睡眠'，后者是前者的具体例子，所以用For example（例如）。判断技巧：看前后句关系，后句是对前句的具体化、实例化。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Reading is like exercising for your brain. It opens up new worlds and improves your language skills. (17)_____, many students find it hard to read regularly. Here are some tips to help you.

(18)_____, choose books that interest you. If you love science, read about discoveries and inventions. Don't force yourself to read boring books just because they are "classic."

Next, set aside a specific time for reading every day. (19)_____, reading for just 20 minutes before bed can help you relax and sleep better. Consistency is more important than reading for long hours once in a while.

(20)_____, discuss what you read with friends or family. Sharing ideas makes reading more fun and helps you understand the book better.
questions.explanation: 本题考查段落衔接与递进关系。前文已经讲了'选感兴趣的书'和'每天固定时间读'两条建议，这里讲'与他人讨论所读内容'，是第三条建议，表递进补充，用In addition（此外、另外）。也可以用Finally，但选项中没有，In addition表示在已有建议基础上增加一条，符合语境。
questions.label: 短文填空
